name: Lenny Bruce
abstract: Leonard Alfred Schneider (October 13, 1925 – August 3, 1966), better known by his stage name Lenny Bruce, was an American stand-up comedian, social critic, and satirist. He was renowned for his open, free-wheeling, and critical style of comedy that combined satire, politics, religion, sex, and vulgarity.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: Lenny Bruce and I did this show called “Stars” or something... So we went on and at this time there was another guy... Lenny and I went on it... We worked there with Lenny
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Dave and I went to visit Lennie in his hotel room... That’s how I got to know Lennie Bruce, and I got to hang out with him for a while
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Because a lot of those comedians back then did a lot of things with musicians, like Lenny Bruce and all these different cats... [INTERVIEWEE] They all could – they all kind of – either they were the star on the show, or else a special star, something like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: I had been engaged many times, to Vidal Sassoon, to Lenny Bruce, etc., etc., both of whom were extraordinary people. Vidal wasn’t Vidal yet. He was trying to get established. Lenny used to send me love letters on airsick bags.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: Working right down the street was Lennie . . . [INTERVIEWER] Lennie Bruce? [INTERVIEWEE] Lennie Bruce. Ciro’s right across the street.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: Then Lenny Bruce came. Lenny Bruce, he had to say a whole bunch of stuff about Marilyn Monroe walking out...
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
